mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls signals ongoing labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following positive payroll growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales signal weakening consumer demand and compress earnings multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during the walk-forward test period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of unemployment above 4% and a federal funds rate above 4% creates restrictive financial conditions that amplify equity downside risk beyond either factor alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.04, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over additive single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals an impending cyclical downturn rather than a temporary supply adjustment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods, month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the risk-free rate and equity risk premium, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad contraction spanning both supply and demand, amplifying downside equity risk beyond single-sector signals and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month and RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of INDPRO and RSAFS declines fails to produce negative 5-day returns with Rank IC below 0.04, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month triggers recession fears and sharp downward revisions to earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in a month.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth captures aggregate demand momentum that is not fully priced into equities at the release horizon, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of quarterly observations over a 5-year window.
